title: Quiz 2 and Final Export

video.note: Uses Quiz 2 as the closing checkpoint, then covers the Deliver page, render settings, single clip export, codec awareness, H.264/H.265/ProRes comparison, file naming, render queue, multiple versions, and final reflection.

learningTarget: I can complete a final checkpoint and export a small edited video with clear naming, useful render settings, and a reflection on my post-production workflow.

bellRinger.prompt: What needs to be true before a project is ready to export for someone else to watch?

vocabulary.definition: The Resolve page used to render or export a timeline into a video file.
To convert the timeline into a finished video file.
A method for compressing and storing video or audio data.
A common delivery codec that is widely compatible for online video.
A newer efficient codec that can make smaller high-quality files but may have compatibility limits.
A high-quality editing and mastering codec that usually creates larger files.
The list of export jobs waiting to render in Resolve.
Rendering the whole timeline as one video file.

assignment.title: Final Resolve Mini-Edit Export
evidenceRequired: Quiz 2 completion record or teacher checkoff.
Google Drive or YouTube link to the final exported mini-edit.
Google Drive link to a screenshot of the Deliver page or render queue.
Short reflection explaining the most important editing, audio, title, or export decision.

exitTicket: What export setting or file naming choice do you need to remember for future video projects?
